common.VADescription: Get guided help with our automated Virtual Assistant
common.hardwareDiagnosticsDescription: Check system health with HP PC Hardware Diagnostics
common.troubleshootingDesc: Find automated diagnostic tools that can help resolve common computer issues like no power no boot, slow pc, sound issues, wireless networking, recovery, and Windows issues. Select your issue from the options below to see solutions for hardware and software problems.
common.issueNotListedDesc: If your issue isn’t listed above, our automated Virtual Assistant can offer guided troubleshooting.
common.likeToKeepVAActiveSession: Would you like to keep your current Virtual Assistant session active in the background?
common.toCloseActiveSession: Would you like to close the active session?
common.selectVAOptionsBelow: Our Virtual Assistant can help you diagnose and solve common computer problems with device power, slow processing, sound, wireless networking, and Windows. Select your issue from the options below.
common.offerMoreGeneralTroubleshooting: If your issue isn’t listed above, HP Virtual Assistant can also offer more general troubleshooting.
common.selectVAOptionsBelowAccount: Our Virtual Assistant can help you diagnose and solve common account issues. Select your topic from the options below.
common.hpOneAccountProblems: HP One - Account problems (trouble logging in to HP One / UCDE)
common.selectVAOptionsBelowMSOneDrive: Our Virtual Assistant can help you diagnose and solve common MS OneDrive issues. Select your topic from the options below.
common.selectVAOptionsBelow5G: Our Virtual Assistant can help you diagnose and solve common 5G connectivity issues. Select your topic from the options below.
common.5GModemServiceSetup: 5G Modem service Setup & switching to WI-FI
common.ifYourIssueIsntListVAOfferMoretourblesho: If your issue isn’t listed above, our Virtual Assistant can also offer more general troubleshooting.
common.leaveSupportVASession: If you leave the Support site, your Virtual Assistant session will close. You will need to start a new session if you still need help.
common.areYouSureCancelVASession: Are you sure you want to end your Virtual Assistant session? You will need to start a new session if you still need help.

common.Battery_Checkbox_Info: I agree to share my device and warranty information with HP to assist in opening a support case.
common.Battery_Remote_Show_Text: Sign in to this device to run Battery check
common.Battery_Why_Weak_Content: The battery is functioning correctly, but due to normal ageing of the battery, the battery life between charges is now significantly shorter than when the battery was new.
common.Sub_Title_In_Testing: HP Battery Check is testing both the primary and secondary battery if your device is equipped with a secondary battery.
common.WaitComplete: It is recommended to let the process complete.
common.GLB_EnableYourPCShowContactOptions: Enable your PC to show contact options, warranty information and important support messages.
common.Storage_Storage_Selftest_PleaseWaitTxt: This may take up to {{minutes}} minutes. Please wait.
common.STORAGE_SIGNIN_REMOTE: Sign in to this device to run storage self-check
common.EAS_DISCLAIMER: I agree to share my device and warranty information with HP to assist in opening a support case.
common.ERR_SessionExpired: Your session has expired, please sign in.
common.AddToProfile: Click the button below to detect and add a new device to your HP Support Assistant profile.
common.AddDeviceView_PleaseWaitTxt: This may take up to {{seconds}} seconds. Please wait.
common.strMsgWeakInWarranty: The battery is functioning correctly, but due to normal ageing of the battery, the battery life between charges is significantly shorter than when the battery was new.  The battery may be covered by your HP Warranty.  Please contact HP.
common.strMsgWeakNotInWarranty: The battery is functioning correctly, but due to normal ageing of the battery, the battery life between charges is now significantly shorter than when the battery was new.  You should consider purchasing a new battery to optimise your computing experience.
common.strMsgVeryWeakInWarranty: The battery is functioning correctly, but due to normal ageing of the battery, the battery life between charges has reached a point where your battery is no longer useful.  The battery may be covered by your HP Warranty.  Please contact HP.
common.strMsgVeryWeakNotInWarranty: The battery is functioning correctly, but due to normal ageing of the battery, the battery life between charges has reached a point where your battery is no longer useful.  You should consider purchasing a new battery to optimise your computing experience.
common.strMsgUnknown: HP Battery Check was unable to access the battery. Please shut down the PC and re-insert the battery and AC Adapter. If this does not correct the problem, please verify that you are using the latest version of HP Battery Check and the latest BIOS version. (See HP Support Assistant or check on www.hp.com/gb-en/home.html for information on software updates.)
common.strUIChoice: Which mode would you like to use?
common.strMsgOKDeep: The battery is functioning correctly, but it has not been charged recently. The battery should be charged completely before removing the AC adapter.
common.strMsgOKLowTemperature: The battery is functioning correctly, but has a low ambient temperature caused by the surrounding environment. The battery should be fully charged before removing the AC adapter.
common.SET_GEN_HPCustExp_Ques: Would you like to help HP improve the user experience by anonymously sharing some computer usage data with us?
common.strMsgOK: The Battery Status is an indicator of how well the multiple cells for the battery are charging and discharging. A Normal Status indicates the battery is functioning correctly.
common.strMsgOKNoAC: The battery is functioning correctly, but the AC adapter is not attached. To completely test your battery, please attach the AC adapter.
common.strMsgOKMulti: The battery is functioning correctly, but your system has two batteries installed. To completely test your batteries, ensure that the AC adapter is inserted and remove one of the batteries. After the results are determined, remove the tested battery and insert the other battery.
common.strMsgOKTemp: The battery is functioning correctly, but the battery is operating at a high temperature and is not charging. Please check the battery again after 30 minutes or when the battery has returned to a normal operating temperature.
common.strMsgCalibrate: The battery is functioning correctly, but it needs to be calibrated. You will experience shorter battery life between charges if the battery is not calibrated.
common.strMsgReplaceChargeCapacity: Based on the charge capacity of your battery it has reached the end of its useful life. You should replace the battery at your earliest convenience.
common.strMsgWarrantyNote: Note: This result is not covered by warranty.
common.strMsgReplaceInWarranty: The battery is reporting a failure and needs to be replaced as soon as possible. Your battery may be covered by your HP Warranty. Please contact HP.
common.strMsgReplaceOutOfWarranty: The battery is reporting a failure and needs to be replaced as soon as possible. Based on the age of your PC and battery, it appears it is no longer covered by your HP Warranty.
common.strMsgNoBatteryPrimary: A battery was not detected. If a battery is installed, please re-insert the battery and AC adapter to retest the battery.
common.strMsgNoBatterySecondary: A battery was not detected, but your system supports a second battery. You may want to purchase a second battery to extend your battery life between charges.
common.strMsgError: An error was encountered trying to access the battery. Please re-insert the battery and the AC Adapter to retest the battery.
common.strFulfillmentAccessories: Click here to purchase HP power accessories
common.strFulfillmentReplacement: Click here to receive a replacement battery from HP
common.strFulfillmentPurchReplacement: Click here to purchase a replacement battery
common.AboutMessage1: HP Battery Check is an HP software application which helps you to check if your battery is functioning properly.
common.AboutMessage2: HP Battery Check will tell you the status of your battery and provide you with methods to replace your battery if necessary.
common.strMessagePurchaseSecond: Click here to purchase a second battery
common.strMsgCalibrateLearn: Click here to learn how to calibrate your battery.
common.strMsgEnableCalibrate: Enable Battery Calibration in HP Tune-Up
common.strAboutForm: HP Battery Check \n \n Copyright 2006-2007 HP Development company, L P
common.strCalibrating: For more information about calibrating the battery please click here.
common.strMessageBadCell: One or more of the cells in the battery are not functioning properly. Please contact HP for warranty service options.
common.strMessageBatFailure: HP battery check has detected a failure.
common.strMessageCalib: This battery needs to be calibrated. Charge it up to 99-100%, then discharge it completely.
common.strMessageCapacityLow: The storage capacity of the battery is low. This is a normal condition. HP recommends purchasing a replacement battery at this time.
common.strMessageChargeInfo: The Battery charge is the percentage of the current charge remaining on the battery.
common.strMessageChargeProb: The battery is not charging properly. Please Contact HP to schedule a repair.
common.strMessageHealthInfo: The Battery health is the indicator of how well the multiple cells for the battery are charging and discharging. A Good Status indicates the battery is functioning properly.
common.strMessageNeedAC: The Battery Health status is not valid when the AC power adapter is not attached. Please insert the AC power adapter and wait a few seconds for the status to be updated.
common.strMessageNeedCharge: The Battery Health status is not valid when the battery is not charging. Please unplug and reinsert the AC adapter to ensure that the battery is charging.
common.strMessageNoBat: The Primary battery was not detected. Check to ensure that the battery is properly inserted then restart the computer.
common.strMessageNoDetect: The Battery Health could not be determined. Please remove the AC power adapter, wait 5 seconds, then re-insert it into the Notebook PC.
common.strMessageNowarranty: Based on the age of your battery, it is no longer covered by your HP Warranty.
common.strMessagePurchPrimary: Click here to purchase a replacement battery.
common.strMessagePurchSecond: Click here to purchase a secondary battery.
common.strMessageRemBat:
Battery Health status is not valid when more than one battery is installed. Please perform the following steps. 
	1. Remove the AC adapter
	2. Remove one of the batteries
	3. Insert the AC adapter
common.strMessageSupPrimary: The Primary battery was not detected. Your system supports more than one battery.
common.strMessageSupSecond: The Secondary battery was not detected. Your system supports more than one battery.
common.strMessageWarranty: Click here to contact HP for warranty service options.
common.strRerun: The Battery Health could not be determined. Please remove the AC power adapter, wait 5 seconds, then re-insert it into the notebook PC.
common.strTestingMessage: Your batteries are being tested. Please wait.....
common.Error_dual_account: We’re Sorry! Battery Check can only be run by one user at a time. Please switch to the user who is currently running Battery Check and close the program before you start Battery Check on the other user you choose.
common.Error_guest_account: We’re Sorry! You are attempting to run Battery Check under a Guest Account. Battery Check is designed to run in an Administrator or Standard User Account type. Please switch to a user account with administrator privileges to run Battery Check.
common.Counterfeit_des_1: HP recommends using HP batteries only. Using a non-compatible or non-HP battery might pose health and safety risks. The installed battery does not appear to be an HP battery and fast charging might not be available.
common.Counterfeit_des_2: Get a genuine HP battery to restore the performance of your notebook.

common.txt_instantink_Desc: HP Instant Ink replacement service is available for select Envy and OfficeJet printer models. Select your plan and we take care of the rest. Never run out of ink and printing will cost a lot less.

common.PrinterOfflineIssues_Desc: If your printer is currently turned on but shows as offline while trying to print, this tool can help resolve this problem.
common.GLB_Warranty_Reseller: Product warranty coverage is provided by the product reseller, please contact them for support.
common.GLB_Warranty_Reseller2: Product was exchanged under warranty, please validate your serial number/product number and contact your reseller for support.
common.GLB_Warranty_EndOfService: Product has reached the end of its support life, no service is available for product.
common.GLB_Warranty_Reseller3: Your printer has exceeded the maximum page count covered under warranty.
common.HPSmartFriendDesc: Get phone access to trained technicians who provide assistance regarding the use of most popular software applications, wired or wireless network setup, operating system upgrades, the software installation process, the migration of data to a new PC and more.
common.TPSWarranty_Desc: Regain phone access to a certified HP technician
common.HPRecoveryKits_Desc: Recover and speed up your HP device
common.txt_launchHpSmart_Desc: Setup, print, scan and share files by using HP Smart with your HP Printer. HP Smart makes it easy to get started and keeps you going with features like Print Anywhere or Mobile Fax!
